page: Amazon Web Services c8ib.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server instance powered by Intel x86_64 processors. It features 192 virtual CPUs mapped to 96 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The CPU resources are fully dedicated to the instance. The server is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance.

For network connectivity, the instance provides a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps. It does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal architecture eliminates virtualization overhead, maximizing the utility of the 192 dedicated vCPUs. However, the lack of local storage requires external block storage solutions, which may impact overall architectural complexity and storage-related costs.

This hardware profile is engineered for compute-intensive workloads that require high-frequency, dedicated CPU execution and high network throughput. Suitable use cases include high-performance computing (HPC), large-scale batch processing, distributed analytics, and compute-heavy scientific simulations.
description: Amazon Web Services c8ib.metal-48xl is an x86_64 bare-metal server designed for compute-intensive workloads. It features 192 dedicated vCPUs across 96 physical Intel cores running at 3.9 GHz, paired with 384.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a compute-optimized memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying instead on external storage solutions. It delivers a high-capacity network baseline of 200 Gbps. The bare-metal deployment model eliminates virtualization overhead, making it highly efficient for raw CPU execution. This server is suited for high-performance computing, batch processing, and distributed simulation workloads that demand dedicated processor access and high network throughput.
